article_title: Champlain Hudson Power Express Begins Delivering Canadian Hydropower to New York City Over a 339-Mile Buried Line
summary: Core CHPE story fully corroborated, but two subordinate specifics could not be verified — the summary's 'June 1' delivery date (absent from the body and all accessible snapshots) and the $97.50/MWh strike price (sole-sourced to the bot-blocked S&P Global). Filed as corrections.

findings.message: The $97.50/MWh first-year strike price is sourced only to S&P Global, which returned HTTP 403 to both the snapshot fetcher and a live WebFetch, and is not present in the accessible Energy Central republication snapshot (a JavaScript-heavy page that captured only the lead). The figure could not be independently verified.
findings.details: https://www.spglobal.com/energy/en/news-research/latest-news/electric-power/051326-1250-mw-chpe-transmission-line-into-nyc-reaches-commercial-operation-early — recoverable: a subordinate financial detail honestly attributed to S&P; flagged because it cannot be verified from any accessible source. Filed as a correction.

findings.message: The summary states the line 'began contract deliveries June 1', but 'June 1' appears nowhere in the article body and in none of the accessible source snapshots (Utility Dive, Energy Central, Wikipedia). The body says only that the line reached commercial operation ahead of schedule (S&P's report is dated May 13) and was 'not expected until June'.
findings.details: Recoverable: the summary's core claims (1,250 MW HVDC reaching commercial operation ahead of schedule, ~20% of NYC's power, Hydro-Québec) are all corroborated; only the specific 'June 1' delivery-start date is unsupported by the cited accessible sources. Filed as a correction.

content_preview.title: Champlain Hudson Power Express Begins Delivering Canadian Hydropower to New York City Over a 339-Mile Buried Line
content_preview.summary: The 1,250 MW HVDC line reached commercial operation ahead of schedule and began contract deliveries June 1, capable of carrying roughly 20% of NYC's power from Hydro-Québec.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Champlain Hudson Power Express (CHPE), a 1,250-megawatt high-voltage direct current transmission line, reached commercial operation ahead of schedule, clearing the way for Canadian hydropower to flow into New York City, according to [Energy Central](https://www.energycentral.com/energy-management/post/news-1-250-mw-chpe-transmission-line-into-nyc-reaches-commercial-rhy8cNG1mkHq3cc), which republished reporting from [S&P Global](https://www.spglobal.com/energy/en/news-research/la...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-organized infrastructure News piece (554 words) on the CHPE line reaching commercial operation, with no direct quotes (the bot deliberately dropped 403'd/off-allowlist quotes and figures).
editor_notes.source_verification: Three of four snapshots read (HTTP 200); S&P Global returned 403. source-0 (Utility Dive) confirms the 1,250 MW rating, the 'more than 300 miles' length, the Hertel-to-Astoria-Annex route, the 25-year contract with the New York State Energy Research and Development Authority, the 10.4 TWh/year, the Transmission Developers Inc. / Blackstone / CHPE LLC ownership, the FERC OATT/MACAS tariff approvals, the 'first merchant transmission … physical reservations' characterization, and the $1.65/month ratepayer figure. source-2 (Energy Central, republishing S&P) confirms the ~20% of NYC power, the 'reaches commercial operation early', and 'Operation wasn't expected until June'. source-3 (Wikipedia) confirms the '339 mi (546 km)' total length (supporting the headline), the 2022 construction start, and the Lake Champlain / Hudson / Harlem / East River route. source-1 (S&P Global) is bot-blocked (403 to both the snapshot fetcher and live WebFetch).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: The core story and the headline's 339-mile figure are fully corroborated. Two subordinate specifics could not be verified: (1) the summary's 'began contract deliveries June 1' — 'June 1' is absent from the body and from every accessible snapshot; (2) the $97.50/MWh first-year strike price — sole-sourced to the bot-blocked S&P Global and not present in the accessible Energy Central republication. Both are filed as corrections. No claim in the verified body is fabricated.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; 'What We Don't Know' appropriately scopes the dispatch and future-transmission uncertainties.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior CHPE coverage in the archive.
concerns: Summary contains an unsourced 'June 1' date; a body strike-price figure rests on a bot-blocked source — both filed as corrections.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid, well-sourced core; APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS for the two unverifiable subordinate specifics (the summary's June 1 date and the S&P-only strike price).
